---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Wilson Cam"
date: "2025-03-11"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r}
library(zoo)
library(tseries)
library(forecast)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```


```{r}
df <- read.csv("/Users/judynei/Downloads/multiTimeline.csv", 
               stringsAsFactors = FALSE, 
               skip = 2)  # Skip first 2 rows
colnames(df) <- c("Month", "Temp")
```

```{r}
# Find outliers using boxplot.stats
outlier_values <- boxplot.stats(df$Temp)$out

# Count of outliers
length(outlier_values)

# Identify the lower and upper bounds for outlier detection
Q1 <- quantile(df$Temp, 0.25)
Q3 <- quantile(df$Temp, 0.75)
IQR <- Q3 - Q1
lower_bound <- Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
upper_bound <- Q3 + 1.5 * IQR

# Removing outliers using explicit condition
df_clean <- df[df$Temp >= lower_bound & df$Temp <= upper_bound, ]

# Boxplots before and after removing outliers
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))  # Display two plots side by side

boxplot(df$Temp, main = "Boxplot of Search Interest (Before Removing Outliers)", 
        ylab = "Temperature", col = "lightblue")

boxplot(df_clean$Temp, main = "Boxplot of Search Interest (After Removing Outliers)", 
        ylab = "Temperature", col = "lightgreen")
```


```{r}
ggplot(df, aes(x = Temp)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Histogram of Search Interest",
       x = "Search Interest",
       y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Convert to time series object (monthly data, starts in January 2004)
df_ts <- ts(na.omit(df_clean$Temp), start = c(2004, 1), frequency = 12)

summary(df_ts)

rolling_window <- 12  

# Compute rolling standard deviation
rolling_sd <- rollapply(df_ts, width = rolling_window, FUN = sd, fill = NA, align = "right")

# Create a data frame for ggplot
rolling_sd_df <- data.frame(
  Time = time(df_ts),
  Rolling_SD = rolling_sd
)

# Plot Rolling Standard Deviation
ggplot(rolling_sd_df, aes(x = Time, y = Rolling_SD)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue", size = 1) +
  ggtitle("Rolling Standard Deviation of Temperature Search Interest") +
  xlab("Time") + 
  ylab("Rolling Standard Deviation") +
  theme_minimal()

# Perform Augmented Dickey-Fuller Test for stationarity
adf_result <- adf.test(df_ts)

# Print ADF test results
print(adf_result)

# Plot the raw time series
plot(df_ts, type = "l", col = "blue",
     main = "Raw Monthly Temperature Search Interest Time Series",
     xlab = "Time", ylab = "Search Interest")
```
```{r}
rolling_window <- 12  

# Compute the rolling mean
rolling_mean <- rollmean(df_ts, k = rolling_window, fill = NA, align = "right")

# Create a time series plot with rolling mean
autoplot(df_ts, series = "Original", color = "blue") +
  autolayer(rolling_mean, series = "Rolling Mean", color = "red") +
  ggtitle("Rolling Mean of Temperature Search Interest") +
  xlab("Time") + 
  ylab("Search Interest") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
df_ts <- log(df_ts)
plot(df_ts, type = "l", col = "blue", main = "Log Transformed Time Series")

```
```{r}
par(mfrow = c(1,2))  # Arrange plots side by side
acf(df_ts, main = "ACF of Search Interest")
pacf(df_ts, main = "PACF of Search Interest")
```



```{r}
df_ts <- diff(df_ts, lag = 12)  # monthly differencing
plot(df_ts, main = "Seasonally Differenced AEP Load Data")
```


```{r}
# Decompose the time series (multiplicative or additive)
decomp <- decompose(df_ts, type = "additive")  # Use "additive" if appropriate

plot(decomp$trend, main = "Trend Component", col = "blue", ylab = "Temperature")
plot(decomp$seasonal, main = "Seasonal Component", col = "green", ylab = "Seasonality")
plot(decomp$random, main = "Residual Component", col = "red", ylab = "Residuals")
```

```{r}
acf(na.omit(decomp$random), main = "ACF of Residuals")
pacf(na.omit(decomp$random), main = "PACF of Residuals")

arma_model <- arima(na.omit(decomp$random), order = c(3, 0, 1))
summary(arma_model)

Box.test(residuals(arma_model), lag = 12, type = "Ljung-Box")

```

```{r}
# Decompose time series (assuming ts_data is your original time series)
decomp <- decompose(df_ts)

# Extract components
trend <- decomp$trend
seasonality <- decomp$seasonal
residuals <- na.omit(decomp$random)  # Remove NAs from residuals

# Forecast trend using linear model
trend_ts <- ts(na.omit(trend), frequency = 12)  # Adjust frequency if needed
trend_model <- tslm(trend_ts ~ trend)  # Linear trend model
trend_forecast <- forecast(trend_model, h = 12)  # Forecast 12 months ahead

# Extend seasonality by repeating past cycles
seasonal_pattern <- tail(seasonality, 12)  # Last year’s seasonality
seasonal_forecast <- rep(seasonal_pattern, length.out = 12)  # Repeat

# Plot trend forecast
autoplot(trend_forecast) + ggtitle("Trend Forecast")

# Combine trend and seasonal forecasts
final_forecast <- trend_forecast$mean + seasonal_forecast

# Convert to time series for visualization
final_forecast_ts <- ts(final_forecast, start = end(df_ts)[1] + c(0,1), frequency = 12)

# Plot the final forecast
autoplot(final_forecast_ts) +
  ggtitle("Final Forecast: Trend + Seasonality") +
  xlab("Time") + ylab("Predicted Values")
```
```{r}
sarima_model <- Arima(df_ts, order = c(3,0,1), seasonal = c(1,1,1))  # Adjust (P,D,Q)
summary(sarima_model)
acf(residuals(sarima_model), main = "ACF of Residuals")
pacf(residuals(sarima_model), main = "PACF of Residuals")

Box.test(residuals(sarima_model), lag = 12, type = "Ljung-Box")
```
```{r}

```
